title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Dickens, Charles
summary: A Tale of Two Cities opens by contrasting turbulent eras in England and France, describing extremes of wealth and suffering, superstition, and brutal punishments. Dickens personifies Fate as a Woodman and Death as a Farmer preparing tumbrils. He depicts crime, public executions, and social decay in London. The narrative then follows a Dover mail on Shooter’s Hill, where a misty night, wary passengers, a guard with a blunderbuss, and a mysterious messenger converge. Mr. Jarvis Lorry is sought by a despatch from Tellson’s, setting in motion a strange, suspenseful journey that links personal fates to wider revolutionary forces and consequences.

characters.lifespan: alive in the excerpt; pursued by a despatch and continues his journey

characters.lifespan: said to have been laid a dozen years earlier

characters.name: The Youth (executed)
characters.personality: victim of religious intolerance in the narrative
characters.lifespan: sentenced to have his hands cut off, tongue torn with pincers, and body burned alive

characters.name: The Woodman (Fate)
characters.lifespan: metaphorical figure preparing fated ends (told as working unheeded)

characters.name: The Farmer (Death)
characters.lifespan: metaphorical figure associated with Revolution's tumbrils

characters.name: Lord Mayor of London (unnamed)
characters.personality: illustrates vulnerability of authority; publicly humiliated
characters.lifespan: despoiled by a highwayman at Turnham Green (event related in excerpt)

characters.name: The Captain (the highwayman)
characters.personality: bold, predatory, double-faced (City tradesman by day, robber by night)
characters.lifespan: described as committing daring robberies and sometimes escaping after violence

characters.name: The Hangman
characters.personality: busy, ruthless, an instrument of state violence
characters.lifespan: constantly employed carrying out executions; active in the social scene

characters.name: Wretched Pilferer (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: mentioned as being executed for a small theft in the social commentary

characters.name: Atrocious Murderer (unnamed)
characters.personality: seriously criminal, used as example for capital punishment
characters.lifespan: noted as being executed in the public sequence described

characters.name: Prisoners of London Gaols (collective)
characters.personality: depicted fighting turnkeys and suffering under the law
characters.lifespan: subject to public punishment and execution as part of social order
